mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, ideal for teams looking to implement AI-driven applications. It provides a robust set of features for self-hosting, making it suitable for organizations that require control over their infrastructure. Key trade-offs include the need for proper resource allocation and management to ensure optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.langgenius.com

```

> This URL points to the official Dify Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) need to be installed for Dify to function properly. This step ensures that all necessary CRDs are set up in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command fetches and applies the CRDs from the Dify repository.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Dify in the specified namespace with the desired version. Adjust the namespace as needed.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace '0.12.0' with the desired version if necessary.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Dify, which is necessary to start using the platform.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  exampleField: exampleValue
EOF

```

> This command creates a simple Dify resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  exampleField: exampleValue
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Dify Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get dify -n dify

```

> You should see the 'example-dify' resource listed with a status of 'Ready'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes Dify from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete crd difys.dify.langgenius.com

```

> This command removes the Dify release from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the Dify CRDs, which will remove all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any critical data. This process involves draining the pods, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

kubectl get dify -n dify

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of the Dify pod to drain it.
> Replace '0.12.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that the Dify resource is in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dify not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify installation may not have been completed successfully.

**Fix:**
Re-run the installation commands and check for errors during the process.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may have already been installed previously.

**Fix:**
Use 'kubectl delete crd difys.dify.langgenius.com' to remove the existing CRDs before re-installing.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate a misconfiguration in the Dify Custom Resource.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to network issues or incorrect kubeconfig settings.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly set up and that you can reach the Kubernetes API server.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new features for agentic workflows, improved API endpoints for better performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy API endpoints that are no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.11, ensure to review the new API changes and update your Custom Resources accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify resource listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get dify -n dify'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have kubectl and Helm installed.
